_comment: Canonical wire envelope for the OPERATOR_HEARTBEAT_RECEIVED SSE event pushed from g8ee to g8ed (and fanned out to the browser). Authorship split: g8eo authors the telemetry inside `metrics`; g8ee owns the envelope (`operator_id`, `status`). `metrics` is the same nested OperatorHeartbeat shape that is persisted on the operator document â€” no flat projection, no divergence. Producers: g8ee `HeartbeatSSEEnvelope` (components/g8ee/app/models/operators.py). Consumers: g8ed `EventService` forwards the envelope unchanged to the browser; the browser parses `data.metrics` as a HeartbeatSnapshot (mirror of OperatorHeartbeat).

operator_id.description: Operator identifier. Matches the `operator_id` field on the persisted OperatorDocument.

status.description: Authoritative operator status at the time g8ee emitted the envelope. See status.json operator.status. g8ee is the sole writer â€” never derive from heartbeat fields.

metrics.description: The persisted OperatorHeartbeat instance, serialized as-is. Canonical shape: shared/models/wire/heartbeat.json#operator_heartbeat (nested: system_identity, performance, network, uptime, version_info, os_details, user_details, disk_details, memory_details, environment, plus top-level fingerprint/capability flags).

_comment.ownership: Authorship boundary: HeartbeatSSEEnvelope is g8ee-owned (`operator_id` + `status`); `metrics` is the g8eo-authored OperatorHeartbeat snapshot, unchanged from its persisted form. Mutating `status` on the envelope after construction is explicitly disallowed â€” the authoritative value comes from OperatorDocument at the call site in heartbeat_service.py.
_comment.routing: Routing fields (`web_session_id`, `user_id`, `case_id`, `investigation_id`) are added by the surrounding SessionEvent/BackgroundEvent envelope in app/models/events.py. They are NOT part of this wire schema and must never be placed inside `metrics`.
_comment.alignment: Alignment rule: the frontend HeartbeatSnapshot model must mirror OperatorHeartbeat exactly. There is no flat projection and no fallback reads â€” adding flat-key accessors at the call site is prohibited per docs/developer.md (No Deduplicators, Arbitrary Guards, or Unions).
